behavior_summary: This script implements the behaviour for a 'screamer' game object with two visible behaviors: a short audible/text response when looked at, and multi-path hit handling when damaged.

U8P_SCREAMER::SCREAMER::look: when the object is inspected/entered by an actor (inferred Event 0 / enterFastArea), it emits a bark/voice string "screamer" directed at the triggering process/player and then suspends — a simple attention/description reaction.

U8P_SCREAMER::SCREAMER::gotHit: on hit events the handler branches: if the incoming control flag indicates an early/ignored hit it returns; otherwise it tests whether the target reference is an NPC. If not an NPC it destroys the item. If it is an NPC it has two main paths: for a specific hit-type (inferred == 1) it plays a scream SFX and applies damage equal to the NPC's current HP (via Npc::getHp -> Item::receiveHit) and then resets a kernel reference; for other hit-types it teleports the NPC to random coordinates, marks it dead (Npc::setDead), and invokes cleanup/free routines (FREE::2A56) multiple times.
notable_control_flow: U8P_SCREAMER::SCREAMER::look: linear flow — set object info/exclusion, call Item::bark(targetProcess, "screamer"), free string, capture return value and suspend.
U8P_SCREAMER::SCREAMER::gotHit: early-return behavior if the incoming hit flag/word is non-zero (interpreted as 'ignore' or a different hit class).
U8P_SCREAMER::SCREAMER::gotHit: branch on Item::isNpc(target) — non-NPC -> Item::destroy(target); NPC -> further branching by hit-type value.

U8P_SCREAMER::SCREAMER::gotHit: hit-type==1 path applies a scream SFX, reads NPC HP (Npc::getHp) and calls Item::receiveHit with that HP (effectively killing/consuming the NPC), then calls Kernel::resetRef.

U8P_SCREAMER::SCREAMER::gotHit: other-NPC-hit path generates random coordinates (urandom), teleports the NPC (Npc::teleport), marks it dead (Npc::setDead), and runs FREE::2A56 with several codes for cleanup/freeing.
open_questions: Whether Event 0 is strictly the 'U8P_SCREAMER::look' opcode or also fired via enterFastArea triggers in all engine contexts (inferred from project fast-area semantics).
Exact meanings of the numeric parameters passed into SCREAMER::gotHit (the uword and word): inferred roles are attacker/source id and hit-type/flags/damage, but not certain.
Precise semantics and meanings of the numeric codes passed to FREE::2A56 in the teleport/death path (inferred as cleanup/free codes).
Whether the hit-type test uses value==1 to represent a particular weapon/type/effect or a script-specific 'instant kill' flag (inferred but not confirmed).
Whether Kernel::resetRef targets a particular engine timer or process reference associated with the NPC, and what side-effects that has on subsequent AI/processing.

main_function.name: U8P_SCREAMER::look
main_function.purpose: Produce a short audible/text response ('screamer') directed at the triggering process/player when the object is examined or its trigger area is entered, then suspend processing for the object.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_SCREAMER::look/examine) or equivalent area-enter trigger (enterFastArea) from the engine that passes the calling process/player pointer.

function_map.name: U8P_SCREAMER::look
function_map.role: Look/examine handler that barks the literal string "screamer" to the caller and suspends.

function_map.name: U8P_SCREAMER::gotHit
function_map.role: Hit/damage event handler: distinguishes non-NPC vs NPC, provides a scream-and-receiveHit path and a teleport/death cleanup path; destroys non-NPC items.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine routine used to display/emit a short text/voice string from an item/process to a target process/player.

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: Engine query to determine whether a referenced item is an NPC instance (used to branch hit behavior).

function_map.name: U8P_SCREAMER::playSFX
function_map.role: Play a sound effect (used here for a scream/impact sound in the hit-type==1 path).

function_map.name: Npc::getHp
function_map.role: Return NPC current HP (used to compute damage applied via Item::receiveHit).

function_map.name: Item::receiveHit
function_map.role: Apply damage/effects to an item/NPC (called with attacker/source, flags, damage word, context).

function_map.name: Kernel::resetRef
function_map.role: Reset or refresh a kernel/process reference or timer related to the NPC after receiveHit.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Generate uniform random values (used to compute random teleport coordinates).

function_map.name: Npc::teleport
function_map.role: Teleport/move the NPC to given coordinates (used in teleport/death path).

function_map.name: Npc::setDead
function_map.role: Mark the NPC as dead (used after teleport in the non-hit-type-1 death path).

function_map.name: FREE::2A56
function_map.role: Cleanup/free routine invoked multiple times with codes and the NPC reference (likely removes effects, spawn debris, or free resources).

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Destroy non-NPC items (used when Item::isNpc returns false in gotHit).

external_calls.purpose: Emit the label/voice string "screamer" to the calling process/player.
Test whether the hit target is an NPC to decide destroy vs NPC-specific logic.
Destroy the item when it is not an NPC.
Play scream/impact sound in the hit-type==1 path.
Obtain the NPC's current HP to use as the damage amount passed to Item::receiveHit (to finish/kill it).
Apply damage/effect to the NPC (called with attacker/source, flags, and damage equal to current HP for the instant-death path).
Reset/refresh a kernel/process reference or timer after applying the receiveHit effect.
Generate random coordinates (range 0x2710 inferred) for the teleport destination in the non-hit-type-1 death path.
Teleport the NPC to generated random coordinates as part of the death/escape/debris path.
Mark the NPC dead after teleporting in the non-hit-type-1 path.
Invoke cleanup/free routines with several codes and the NPC reference after the teleport/death path.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the behaviour for a 'screamer' game object with two visible behaviors: a short audible/text response when looked at, and multi-path hit handling when damaged.
